creator: jcarchambeau@gmail.com
text:
Kernel crashes after the fpa driver is probed at bootup.  Attached is a
verbose boot demonstrating the issue.  I can replicate this issue on one
of my systems with an Abit BP6 system board or one of my ALR Revolution
2X (NS7000) systems running 6.1.

-- cut here --
/boot/kernel/kernel text=0x4316fc data=0x84ee0+0x5612c syms=[0x4+0x58850+0x4+0x6cf86]

Fatal trap 12: page fault while in kernel mode
cpuid = 0; apic id = 00
fault virtual address	= 0x20
fault code		= supervisor write, page not present
instruction pointer	= 0x20:0xc0661461
stack pointer	        = 0x28:0xc0c20b34
frame pointer	        = 0x28:0xc0c20b34
code segment		= base 0x0, limit 0xfffff, type 0x1b
			= DPL 0, pres 1, def32 1, gran 1
processor eflags	= interrupt enabled, resume, IOPL = 0
current process		= 0 (swapper)
trap number		= 12
panic: page fault
cpuid = 0
Uptime: 1s
Automatic reboot in 15 seconds - press a key on the console to abort

Fix: 

You have one of these options as FDDI support is obviously broken in 6.1.

1. Don't compile FDDI support and the fea/fpa drivers into the 6.1 kernel.
2. Remove the FDDI NIC after FDDI support and fea/fpa is compiled into a
   6.1 kernel.
3. Downgrade to 5.5-RELEASE which does have working FDDI support.  If the
   machine is running 5.4 or 5.5 FDDI functions without issue.

Issue may also mainfest itself with the fea driver as well as it's the same
card, just with an EISA bus.  I haven't tested that with one of my ALR
EISA/PCI machines yet.  I have a pair of DEC FEA cards as well.  I can
provide the same verbose boot log for an EISA/PCI bus machine (ALR Rev
2X or Rev 2XL should one be needed.
How-To-Repeat: Install 6.1 release, enable FDDI support and the fpa or fea driver.
